title: Participarea Președintelui la Consiliul de Pace al lui Donald Trump – opinii pro și contra
summary: The article compiles diverse opinions on Romanian President Nicușor Dan's decision to attend Donald Trump's Board of Peace summit in Washington as an observer. Analysts debate whether this was a pragmatic move driven by national security interests or a misguided step that places Romania in embarrassing company alongside Hungary's Viktor Orbán. The discussion touches on Romania's lack of preparedness for concrete peacekeeping contributions and the broader tension between realism and moral principles in foreign policy.
talking_points: Romania's geographic proximity to the Ukraine war theater and dependence on US military infrastructure (Deveselu shield, American troops) justify pragmatic engagement with the Trump administration
Romania coordinated its observer participation with European partners including Italy, Cyprus (EU presidency holder), and the European Commission, not acting unilaterally
Romania came to the Board of Peace without any concrete military or peacekeeping contribution offer, unlike Italy which proposed carabinieri troops
Critics argue the observer status places Romania in embarrassing company with Hungary's Orbán and signals accommodationism rather than principled foreign policy
Supporters contend that Romania cannot afford to antagonize the US regardless of who leads it, given direct Russian threats at its border
concepts: foreign policy realism vs moral principles
related_concepts: small state diplomacy in great power competition
author: Redacția
